item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Unidentified): Does the dark moon do the dirty chicken?

Alpha: Yes, but I prefer the electric slide.

Easiest upvote of my life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love me some Alexylva, there's not enough of it around these days. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fabulous! Just the right level of zany and technical blended for the life of the article.

Normally, I hate it when SCPs have a giant list of -# instances and each of them are different but you've exercised phenomenal restraint here in your description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Done. Thanks!

"Cyborg Dodo Offspring" is my new band name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: “As children of SCP-4980…” Isn’t “children” humanizing? “Offspring” or something like that would sound more clinical.

item.title: Re: help plz
item.content:encoded:
I always go to this post to see how to do the collapsibles:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-872365/collapsible-blocks-how

(Make sure you post about your idea in the Ideas and Brainstorming forum before making a draft!)

item.title: help plz
item.content:encoded: hey, i'm trying to work on my first SCP and id like to know how you made the audio log collapsible. if you could help that would be awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see Alexylva birb, I do the updoot

In all seriousness though, +1 from me here. I love these sorts of quirky premises, and I wish my university had a team of biologically, mechanically, and mentally enhanced dodos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that is definitely why we made the hub, haha yes

item.title: Caw, caw!
item.content:encoded: I said this before in the critique, but the story is excellent. Not only is it a brilliant way to connect Alexylva, which we always need more of, but the extra story of the Dodo Fight Ring is both funny and a bit sad. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a really good relationship and if I remember correctly the Foundation has a few SCPs being taken care of by WWS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's a good idea! I might edit in something like that later after I've had time to think about it. What's the Foundation's business relationship like with WWS?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A delightfully ridiculous idea executed in a really fun way. It's been a while since an SCP made me grin like an idiot this much. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the late-ish reply.
I know the foundation is cold not cruel but releasing dodos into the wild just doesn't seem like a good idea. They would be an invasive species, no natural predators and they would have to compete for resources less than the natural species. Also wouldn't people find the dodos and wonder why this extinct species is suddenly back in the wild? In my opinion the dodos should probably be given to Wilson's Wildlife Solutions instead, but that's just my two cents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a time to be alive +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, that's why you guys made the hub, right? It was very inspiring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An Alexylva article in 2018? Gosh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fixed the first problem.

As for the second, the Foundation is cold, but not cruel. Non-anomalous dodos are no threat to the foundation, and I'd like to think that the Foundation is dedicated to preserving bio-diversity and are otherwise environmentally conscious.

That's just my personal head-canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
But I have a few questions,

MTF Iota-10 "D*** Feds

Why is damn censored? And why is the Foundation trying to bring back dodos? At this point wouldn't they be an invasive species regardless of where they put them.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Zyn, Modern Erasmus, and Elogee FishTruck for constructive criticism, and to the Alexylva University creators!

Helmet by Luis García, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=3740590
Dodo public domain
Laser module By 彭家杰 - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=32485098
Overall image created by me, CC BY-SA 3.0

More from mathbrush
